title: diodia tetracocca
intro: Diodia tetracocca, also known as swamp pink or pink button, is a flowering plant species native to the southeastern United States. It has been traditionally used in herbal medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antiseptic, and antiviral properties. The plant is believed to have various health benefits, including reducing fever and alleviating symptoms of respiratory infections, as well as exhibiting antimicrobial and antioxidant activities. Diodia tetracocca is often prepared as a tea or tincture, and it can also be used in topical applications to take advantage of its antiseptic properties.
